title: K-Map Reduction Workflow
content_markdown:
1. Mark minterms/maxterms.
2. Form largest power-of-two groups.
3. Prefer overlap only when it reduces literals.
4. Validate with truth table spot checks.

title: FSM Design Checklist
content_markdown: Define states, transitions, reset behavior, and output coding style (Moore/Mealy). Verify unreachable states and default branches.

title: Combinational vs Sequential Blocks
content_markdown: Use `always @(*)` or `always_comb` for combinational logic and `always @(posedge clk ...)` / `always_ff` for sequential logic.

title: Latch Avoidance
content_markdown: Initialize outputs and cover all branches in combinational blocks to avoid unintended latch inference.

title: Setup/Hold Intuition
content_markdown: Setup is max-delay check; hold is min-delay check. Fix setup with faster data path or slower capture clock, fix hold with delay insertion.

title: Essential SDC Commands
content_markdown: Start with `create_clock`, input/output delays, false/multicycle paths, and clock uncertainty before deep optimization.

title: always_ff/always_comb Best Practices
content_markdown: These constructs add tool checks for intent and reduce class of simulation-synthesis mismatches.

title: Packed vs Unpacked
content_markdown: Packed is bit-level contiguous representation; unpacked is array-of-elements representation.

title: Generator-Driver-Monitor-Scoreboard
content_markdown: This decomposition gives separation of concerns and easier debug when coverage or checks fail.

title: Constraint Debugging
content_markdown: When randomization fails, reduce constraint set, inspect conflicting ranges, and incrementally restore intent.

title: Agent Anatomy
content_markdown: An active agent includes sequencer, driver, and monitor. Passive agent typically contains only monitor.

title: Run Phase Objections
content_markdown: Raise objection before traffic starts and drop when done to control test end cleanly.

title: Implication Operators
content_markdown: Use `|->` for overlapped and `|=>` for non-overlapped implication depending on cycle alignment intent.

title: Handshake Assertions
content_markdown: Convert spec timing to temporal properties with clear reset disable and bounded delays where possible.

title: From Floorplan to Route
content_markdown: Typical order: floorplan -> power plan -> placement -> CTS -> route -> signoff checks and ECO.

title: Early Congestion Prevention
content_markdown: Use macro spacing, blockages, balanced utilization targets, and buffering strategy to avoid late-stage congestion crises.

title: Independent Channels
content_markdown: AW, W, B, AR, R channels are independently handshaked, enabling pipelining and higher throughput.

title: When to Use APB vs AHB
content_markdown: APB for simple low-bandwidth peripherals; AHB for higher-performance memory-mapped backbone segments.

title: How to Answer Design Debug Questions
content_markdown: Structure answers as: symptom -> hypothesis -> experiment -> observation -> fix -> prevention.

title: Performance and Power Tradeoff Cases
content_markdown: Prepare examples on clock gating, DVFS assumptions, and protocol bottleneck debugging with measurable metrics.

title: High-Throughput Pipeline Discussion
content_markdown: Expect questions on hazard control, occupancy, buffering, and latency-hiding patterns in compute-heavy pipelines.
